2605.21645.title: AOP-Wiki EMOD 3.0: Data Model Expansions and Content Evaluation Framework for Using Agentic AI to Improve Integration between AOPs and New Approach Methodologies (NAMs)
2605.21645.paper_summary: Adverse Outcome Pathways (AOP) are logic models that causally link biological mechanisms that can be measured in a lab to adverse outcomes, relevant to chemical regulatory endpoints. AOPs contextualize new approach methodologies (NAMs), in vitro and in silico methods used as alternatives to animal testing and the sequential events in an AOP serve as multi-scale models spanning biological scales. The AOP-Wiki serves as the global repository for AOPs. While the AOP-Wiki has played a central role in AOP expansion over the past decade, constraints within the current data model and application infrastructure limit the AOP-Wiki from supporting continued AOP growth and evolution. Yet, the transformative power of agentic AI has re-invigorated AOP-Wiki data modernization efforts at a time when core AOP principles can be harnessed to inform use of AI for aggregating and structuring AOP-relevant information. Seizing upon this momentum, we present AOP-Wiki EMOD 3.0, the third in a series of evidence model prototypes, which concretely demonstrates data model expansions and our vision for how the AOP-Wiki might be transformed to better serve regulatory science and emergent use of AOPs in biomedical and One Health contexts. We aim to lay a foundation to support computationally-generated AOPs and quantitative AOPs (qAOPs) by focussing on solutions for AOP-Wiki internal quality improvement, evidence structuring to enhance AOP FAIRness and AI-readiness, and improved integration between the AOP framework and NAMs to better serve next generation risk assessment.
2605.21645.paper_summary_zh: 不良結果途徑（AOP）是邏輯模型，將可在實驗室中測量的生物機制與不良結果因果鏈接，這些不良結果與化學監管終點相關。AOP為新方法論（NAMs）提供了背景，這些方法是用作動物測試替代的體外和體內方法，AOP中的連續事件作為跨越生物尺度的多尺度模型。AOP-Wiki作為AOP的全球資料庫。雖然AOP-Wiki在過去十年中在AOP擴展中發揮了核心作用，但當前數據模型和應用基礎設施中的限制使得AOP-Wiki無法支持持續的AOP增長和演變。然而，代理人工智慧的變革力量在此時重新激活了AOP-Wiki數據現代化的努力，當核心AOP原則可以被利用來指導AI在聚合和結構化AOP相關信息的使用。抓住這一勢頭，我們提出了AOP-Wiki EMOD 3.0，這是證據模型原型系列中的第三個，具體展示了數據模型擴展以及我們對AOP-Wiki如何轉型以更好地服務於監管科學和在生物醫學及一體健康背景下新興使用AOP的願景。我們旨在奠定基礎，以支持計算生成的AOP和定量AOP（qAOP），專注於AOP-Wiki內部質量改進的解決方案、證據結構化以增強AOP的公平性和AI準備性，以及改善AOP框架與NAMs之間的整合，以更好地服務於下一代風險評估。
2605.21645.author: Virginia K. Hench et.al.
2605.21645.authors: Virginia K. Hench, J. Harry Caufield, Sierra A. T. Moxon, Jason M. O'Brien, Stephen W. Edwards
